Goal id: G6

CANDIDATE MODE — you are one of 2 parallel candidate planners under the generate-N-then-judge scheme (Q100/Q101). Ground yourself read-only (fetch_item("goals","G6"), the answered questions Q37-Q43 under milestone M29, wanbond-fixes.md at the repo root, and the actual repo code), then EMIT a full candidate plan as a single fenced json block per your CANDIDATE-mode contract. Write NOTHING to any ledger; emit no status token.

Key answered-question constraints to honor (read the full Q&A yourself; the SPECIFIC answers Q38/Q39 supersede the generic Q43 recommendation where they conflict):
- Q37: gate ONLY I6's 0.0.0.0/0-acceptance on defect D35; everything else plannable now; I3/I4 reference D37/D39 in acceptance without hard dependsOn.
- Q38: I7 = BOTH persistent-tun code AND the C4 oneshot/docs (belt-and-suspenders) — persistent-tun code IS in scope this round.
- Q39: I8 = a bidirectional-standby-liveness verification task INSIDE G6; refile as a defect only if it surfaces one.
- Q40: ship packaged artifacts — NM conf.d drop-in AND templated wanbond-addressing oneshot unit — plus C1/C4 docs referencing them.
- Q41: THIN I6 — daemon applies only the internal /1+/1 allowed_ips split + edge default-route wiring; client-LAN SNAT and concentrator NAT/forwarding stay documented recipes (C3/C6).
- Q42: I5 bind mode = per-path `bind = "source"|"device"|"auto"` on [[paths]] with an optional global default, default `auto` (today's behavior).
- Q43: all docs C1-C6 + I1-I4 + thin I5/I6 in this goal (with Q38/Q39 pulling I7-code and the I8 verification task into scope as answered above).

Every task's ledgerRefs must include "goals:G6". Do not duplicate the D35-D40 fixes themselves (those defects are owned by investigate-flow under M28); tasks may reference them in acceptance text where Q37 says so.

1	# wanbond — production lessons (defects / improvements / docs)
2	
3	Context: real deploy of edge = Raspberry Pi 4 / Debian / **NetworkManager**, concentrator
4	= `o3` (aarch64, OCI, public 89.168.124.91 via NAT from private `enp0s6` 10.0.0.92). Two WAN
5	uplinks arrive at the Pi as VLAN sub-interfaces `eth0.231`(Starlink)/`eth0.232`(5G), each a
6	single-address interface, pinned per-WAN by `ip rule from <source_addr>`. MP client LAN =
7	`eth0.223` (192.168.223.0/24, Pi is .1). Goal reached: a bonded Starlink+5G tunnel to o3
8	(inner 10.77.0.0/24), then VLAN-223 client traffic routed through it and NAT'd out o3.
9	
10	Everything below was observed on real hardware/WANs. Unit + realhosts-P0 tiers are green;
11	these are all gaps that testbed (2 source IPs on ONE interface, both ends started fresh
12	together, never restarted independently) does not exercise.
13	
14	Severity: **S1** = tunnel silently non-functional / multi-minute outage; **S2** = wrong/cryptic
15	behavior with a workaround; **S3** = ergonomics/observability.
16	
17	---
18	
19	## A. DEFECTS (wrong behavior — need a code fix)
20	
21	### D1 (S1) — `allowed_ips = 0.0.0.0/0` wedges the handshake; tunnel never establishes
22	Bisected and confirmed. With the concentrator peer's `allowed_ips = ["0.0.0.0/0"]` on the
23	edge, the WG handshake never completes — even fresh-restarting **both** ends and waiting
24	minutes (edge `tx≈0`, o3 `rx` floods to 2.3 MB with `tx` 9 KB, i.e. o3 receives but never
25	answers; no handshake ever logged). Reverting the same peer to a concrete prefix
26	(`10.77.0.1/32`) establishes in ~25 s, deterministically. Workaround that also works: the
27	**split default** `["0.0.0.0/1","128.0.0.0/1"]`. The virtual-endpoint design (engine never
28	holds the real 89.168.124.91) rules out an endpoint routing-loop, so the cause is a
29	`0.0.0.0/0`-specific path (amneziawg-go allowed-ip trie or a wanbond special-case). **This
30	silently breaks the single most common full-tunnel config.**
31	
32	### D2 (S1) — One-sided restart → multi-minute outage (no prompt re-handshake)
33	Confirmed repeatedly. When **either** end restarts, the peer still holding the old session
34	does not promptly re-handshake; the tunnel stays down for minutes until a rekey timer fires.
35	Restart edge → o3 stale → down for minutes; restart o3 → edge stale → down for minutes; only
36	restarting **both** ~together (both re-initiate from scratch) converges in ~25 s. For a
37	WAN-failover product this is severe — any reboot of the Pi or the concentrator is a
38	multi-minute client outage. Likely fix: force a fresh handshake on startup **and** on the
39	first path-up, and/or have the receiver treat a new valid init from a known static key as
40	immediately superseding the current session.
41	
42	### D3 (S2) — Startup handshake fires before path liveness, then isn't aggressively retried
43	On every start the edge logs one `Failed to send handshake initiation: bind: no healthy
44	path with a known remote endpoint` (paths not up yet), paths transition up ~0.6 s later —
45	and then nothing. The first init is wasted and re-initiation is not visibly driven off the
46	path-up edge, compounding D2. WG should (re)initiate the moment the first path becomes live.
47	
48	### D4 (S1) — Auto device-bind silently defeats source-policy routing
49	`selectDeviceBinds` picks `SO_BINDTODEVICE` (wildcard source) for a one-address/one-path
50	interface — exactly a VLAN-per-WAN edge. A wildcard-source socket never matches `ip rule
51	from <source_addr>`, so the lookup falls to `main` (no route to the concentrator via the
52	VLAN) → ENETUNREACH → silent path-down, zero packets on the wire, while `ping -I
53	<source_ip>` proves the WAN works. Fixed here with `ip rule ... oif <dev> table N`.
54	
55	### D5 (S2) — `wanbond0` left DOWN + unaddressed; NetworkManager then flushes the address
56	The daemon creates the tun but never brings it up or addresses it. On an NM host the tun is
57	auto-managed: NM **flushes** the operator's address on link-up and the interface stays
58	**DOWN** across daemon restarts. Writing to it yields the cryptic `TUN write: input/output
59	error`. Fixed with NM `unmanaged-devices` + an addressing oneshot.
60	
61	### D6 (S3) — `CAP_NET_RAW` (code) vs `CAP_NET_ADMIN` (shipped unit) mismatch
62	`pathsock` says `SO_BINDTODEVICE` needs `CAP_NET_RAW`; the shipped systemd units grant only
63	`CAP_NET_ADMIN`. Per the comment device-bind should always fall back, yet it bound
64	successfully on o3 — so the requirement is kernel-dependent and the comment/unit disagree.
65	Reconcile (grant `CAP_NET_RAW`, or fix the comment).
66	
67	---
68	
69	## B. IMPROVEMENTS (behavior/observability/ergonomics — not strictly a bug)
70	
71	### I1 — Daemon should bring the `wanbond0` link UP
72	It already creates the device; leaving it DOWN is a silent-dead-tunnel footgun (see D5).
73	Link-up is low-risk; addressing can stay operator-owned.
74	
75	### I2 — Expose a session/handshake state signal
76	The single biggest debugging obstacle: `path_up` goes to 1 long before the WG session
77	exists, and the only other signals are raw byte counters. Add `wanbond_session_established`
78	(and/or last-handshake-age) metric + a `handshake complete`/`session up` log line, so
79	"still converging" is distinguishable from "wedged" (D1/D2/D3 all presented identically).
80	
81	### I3 — Actionable error instead of raw `input/output error`
82	On TUN write, check interface state / MTU and emit e.g. `wanbond0 is DOWN — address & bring
83	it up (install.md §4)` rather than EIO (D5).
84	
85	### I4 — Downgrade startup `no healthy path` ERROR spam
86	Logged at ERROR during the normal ~1 s liveness warmup; reads as a hard failure. DEBUG/INFO
87	during warmup, or a single `waiting for path liveness` line (D3).
88	
89	### I5 — Config toggle to force source-IP binding (opt out of device-bind)
90	For source-policy-routing topologies the roam-survival benefit of device-bind is moot and it
91	actively breaks routing (D4). A per-path/global `bind = "source"` switch avoids the whole
92	oif-rule workaround.
93	
94	### I6 — Native full-tunnel support
95	Once D1 is fixed, accept `0.0.0.0/0`; better, let the daemon apply the split internally, or
96	provide a `mode = "default-route"` that wires client routing (see docs C3) so operators
97	don't hand-assemble policy-route + SNAT + concentrator NAT.
98	
99	### I7 — Interface/route persistence across daemon restarts
100	Recreating `wanbond0` on every restart drops every address/route/rule referencing it, forcing
101	an external oneshot to rebuild them each time (addressing, `table 223` default, SNAT). Keep
102	the tun persistent across restarts, or ship the oneshot (see C4).
103	
104	### I8 — Verify standby-path liveness is bidirectional
105	Observed `path_up{5g}=1` with `tx{5g}=0` (edge sends nothing on the standby). Confirm a
106	"up" standby can actually transmit before failover selects it, else failover could pick a
107	path that only proved receive.
108	
109	---
110	
111	## C. DOCUMENTATION UPDATES
112	
113	### C1 — NetworkManager section (install.md §4)
114	Docs are networkd-only; most edge boxes (RPi OS/Debian/Ubuntu desktop) run NM. Add:
115	`unmanaged-devices=interface-name:wanbond0` in `/etc/NetworkManager/conf.d/`, or NM strips
116	the address (D5).
117	
118	### C2 — `source_addr` + device-bind warning
119	State that per-WAN pinning via `ip rule from <source_addr>` is defeated by the auto
120	device-bind, and give the `ip rule add oif <dev> table N` recipe (D4). This is the most
121	common real edge topology and is currently undocumented.
122	
123	### C3 — Full-tunnel / route-a-client-LAN recipe (the primary use case, entirely undocumented)
124	End-to-end: broad `allowed_ips` on the edge (the split, **not** `0.0.0.0/0` until D1 is
125	fixed); on the edge, policy-route the client subnet to `wanbond0` and **SNAT to the tunnel
126	IP** (so the concentrator's `allowed_ips = <edge>/32` still matches) *or* widen the
127	concentrator's `allowed_ips` to the client subnet; on the concentrator, `ip_forward=1`,
128	`MASQUERADE -s <tunnel-net> -o <wan>`, and a `FORWARD` conntrack-ESTABLISHED accept (the
129	shipped `-i wanbond0 ACCEPT` covers only the forward direction — the default FORWARD REJECT
130	rules drop return traffic without it).
131	
132	### C4 — Persistence recipe for non-networkd hosts
133	Bless the `PartOf=wanbond-<role>.service` oneshot pattern that, after the daemon starts,
134	re-applies address + link-up + policy rules + per-table routes + nft SNAT — because all of
135	those die with the recreated interface (I7). Warn that a plain `ExecStartPost` races the
136	tun's creation.
137	
138	### C5 — Expected reconverge window + restart guidance
139	Until D2 is fixed, document that restarting one end can leave the tunnel down for minutes,
140	and that restarting **both** ends ~together is the fast path to reconverge. Include the
141	session-state metric (I2) as the "is it up yet" check.
142	
143	### C6 — Concentrator NAT/forwarding prerequisites
144	Spell out `ip_forward`, `MASQUERADE`, and the `FORWARD` ESTABLISHED accept as required for
145	any routed/full-tunnel use (part of C3, but worth an explicit checklist item).
146	
147	---
148	
149	## What went right (keep)
150	Static single binary; one-binary-two-roles; 0600-config enforcement; the per-path
151	liveness/loss/RTT metrics (indispensable here); netns + realhosts test tiers; the
152	virtual-endpoint multipath design; the transparent-failover datapath itself (once
153	established: 0% loss, ~18–37 ms over the Starlink+5G bond, and VLAN-223 clients egressing via
154	the concentrator's public IP). Every defect above sits at the **restart/re-handshake and
155	operator/edge-plumbing** boundary, not in the steady-state datapath.
156

does not promptly re-handshake; the tunnel stays down for minutes until a rekey timer fires.
Restart edge → o3 stale → down for minutes; restart o3 → edge stale → down for minutes; only
restarting **both** ~together (both re-initiate from scratch) converges in ~25 s. For a
WAN-failover product this is severe — any reboot of the Pi or the concentrator is a
multi-minute client outage. Likely fix: force a fresh handshake on startup **and** on the
first path-up, and/or have the receiver treat a new valid init from a known static key as
immediately superseding the current session.

### D3 (S2) — Startup handshake fires before path liveness, then isn't aggressively retried
On every start the edge logs one `Failed to send handshake initiation: bind: no healthy
path with a known remote endpoint` (paths not up yet), paths transition up ~0.6 s later —
and then nothing. The first init is wasted and re-initiation is not visibly driven off the
path-up edge, compounding D2. WG should (re)initiate the moment the first path becomes live.

### D4 (S1) — Auto device-bind silently defeats source-policy routing
`selectDeviceBinds` picks `SO_BINDTODEVICE` (wildcard source) for a one-address/one-path
interface — exactly a VLAN-per-WAN edge. A wildcard-source socket never matches `ip rule
from <source_addr>`, so the lookup falls to `main` (no route to the concentrator via the
VLAN) → ENETUNREACH → silent path-down, zero packets on the wire, while `ping -I
<source_ip>` proves the WAN works. Fixed here with `ip rule ... oif <dev> table N`.

### D5 (S2) — `wanbond0` left DOWN + unaddressed; NetworkManager then flushes the address
The daemon creates the tun but never brings it up or addresses it. On an NM host the tun is
auto-managed: NM **flushes** the operator's address on link-up and the interface stays
**DOWN** across daemon restarts. Writing to it yields the cryptic `TUN write: input/output
error`. Fixed with NM `unmanaged-devices` + an addressing oneshot.

### D6 (S3) — `CAP_NET_RAW` (code) vs `CAP_NET_ADMIN` (shipped unit) mismatch
`pathsock` says `SO_BINDTODEVICE` needs `CAP_NET_RAW`; the shipped systemd units grant only
`CAP_NET_ADMIN`. Per the comment device-bind should always fall back, yet it bound
successfully on o3 — so the requirement is kernel-dependent and the comment/unit disagree.
Reconcile (grant `CAP_NET_RAW`, or fix the comment).

---

## B. IMPROVEMENTS (behavior/observability/ergonomics — not strictly a bug)

### I1 — Daemon should bring the `wanbond0` link UP
It already creates the device; leaving it DOWN is a silent-dead-tunnel footgun (see D5).
Link-up is low-risk; addressing can stay operator-owned.

### I2 — Expose a session/handshake state signal
The single biggest debugging obstacle: `path_up` goes to 1 long before the WG session
exists, and the only other signals are raw byte counters. Add `wanbond_session_established`
(and/or last-handshake-age) metric + a `handshake complete`/`session up` log line, so
"still converging" is distinguishable from "wedged" (D1/D2/D3 all presented identically).

### I3 — Actionable error instead of raw `input/output error`
On TUN write, check interface state / MTU and emit e.g. `wanbond0 is DOWN — address & bring
it up (install.md §4)` rather than EIO (D5).

### I4 — Downgrade startup `no healthy path` ERROR spam
Logged at ERROR during the normal ~1 s liveness warmup; reads as a hard failure. DEBUG/INFO
during warmup, or a single `waiting for path liveness` line (D3).

### I5 — Config toggle to force source-IP binding (opt out of device-bind)
For source-policy-routing topologies the roam-survival benefit of device-bind is moot and it
actively breaks routing (D4). A per-path/global `bind = "source"` switch avoids the whole
oif-rule workaround.

### I6 — Native full-tunnel support
Once D1 is fixed, accept `0.0.0.0/0`; better, let the daemon apply the split internally, or
provide a `mode = "default-route"` that wires client routing (see docs C3) so operators
don't hand-assemble policy-route + SNAT + concentrator NAT.

### I7 — Interface/route persistence across daemon restarts
Recreating `wanbond0` on every restart drops every address/route/rule referencing it, forcing
an external oneshot to rebuild them each time (addressing, `table 223` default, SNAT). Keep
the tun persistent across restarts, or ship the oneshot (see C4).

### I8 — Verify standby-path liveness is bidirectional
Observed `path_up{5g}=1` with `tx{5g}=0` (edge sends nothing on the standby). Confirm a
"up" standby can actually transmit before failover selects it, else failover could pick a
path that only proved receive.

---

## C. DOCUMENTATION UPDATES

### C1 — NetworkManager section (install.md §4)
Docs are networkd-only; most edge boxes (RPi OS/Debian/Ubuntu desktop) run NM. Add:
`unmanaged-devices=interface-name:wanbond0` in `/etc/NetworkManager/conf.d/`, or NM strips
the address (D5).

### C2 — `source_addr` + device-bind warning
State that per-WAN pinning via `ip rule from <source_addr>` is defeated by the auto
device-bind, and give the `ip rule add oif <dev> table N` recipe (D4). This is the most
common real edge topology and is currently undocumented.

### C3 — Full-tunnel / route-a-client-LAN recipe (the primary use case, entirely undocumented)
End-to-end: broad `allowed_ips` on the edge (the split, **not** `0.0.0.0/0` until D1 is
fixed); on the edge, policy-route the client subnet to `wanbond0` and **SNAT to the tunnel
IP** (so the concentrator's `allowed_ips = <edge>/32` still matches) *or* widen the
concentrator's `allowed_ips` to the client subnet; on the concentrator, `ip_forward=1`,
`MASQUERADE -s <tunnel-net> -o <wan>`, and a `FORWARD` conntrack-ESTABLISHED accept (the
shipped `-i wanbond0 ACCEPT` covers only the forward direction — the default FORWARD REJECT
rules drop return traffic without it).

### C4 — Persistence recipe for non-networkd hosts
Bless the `PartOf=wanbond-<role>.service` oneshot pattern that, after the daemon starts,
re-applies address + link-up + policy rules + per-table routes + nft SNAT — because all of
those die with the recreated interface (I7). Warn that a plain `ExecStartPost` races the
tun's creation.

### C5 — Expected reconverge window + restart guidance
Until D2 is fixed, document that restarting one end can leave the tunnel down for minutes,
and that restarting **both** ends ~together is the fast path to reconverge. Include the
session-state metric (I2) as the "is it up yet" check.

### C6 — Concentrator NAT/forwarding prerequisites
Spell out `ip_forward`, `MASQUERADE`, and the `FORWARD` ESTABLISHED accept as required for
any routed/full-tunnel use (part of C3, but worth an explicit checklist item).

---

## What went right (keep)
Static single binary; one-binary-two-roles; 0600-config enforcement; the per-path
liveness/loss/RTT metrics (indispensable here); netns + realhosts test tiers; the
virtual-endpoint multipath design; the transparent-failover datapath itself (once
established: 0% loss, ~18–37 ms over the Starlink+5G bond, and VLAN-223 clients egressing via
the concentrator's public IP). Every defect above sits at the **restart/re-handshake and
operator/edge-plumbing** boundary, not in the steady-state datapath.

- C4 Persistence recipe for non-networkd hosts — bless the `PartOf=` oneshot pattern (address + link-up + policy rules + per-table routes + nft SNAT after daemon start); warn that a plain ExecStartPost races tun creation (R27 already fixed one instance of that race in the networkd docs).

- C6 Concentrator NAT/forwarding prerequisites checklist (ip_forward, MASQUERADE, FORWARD ESTABLISHED accept).

WHAT WENT RIGHT (keep; do not regress): static single binary; one-binary-two-roles; 0600-config enforcement; per-path liveness/loss/RTT metrics; netns + realhosts test tiers; virtual-endpoint multipath design; the steady-state datapath.

OPEN QUESTIONS FOR CLARIFICATION (planner should ask before designing): sequencing vs the D35-D40 investigations (which improvements are gated on a root cause — e.g. I6 on D35 — vs immediately plannable); whether I7 (tun persistence) is in scope for code or docs-only this round (C4); whether I8 belongs here or as a defect after a quick investigation; whether the NM/oneshot recipes should also ship as packaged unit files vs docs-only; how much of C3's client-LAN wiring should become the I6 `mode="default-route"` automation vs stay documented manual steps.

NON-GOALS: TCP/TLS fallback; protocol mimicry; >3 links; general SD-WAN; GUI; re-opening resolved G1/G2 defects; duplicating the D35-D40 fixes themselves (compose with them).

## wanbond file map for the planning task

Note: I explored the live tree only; ignore the stale copies under `/home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/.claude/worktrees/*` (agent worktrees, not the working repo).

### 1. Config parsing (TOML)
- `/home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/internal/config/config.go` — all structs. `Path` struct at **lines 405-440**: `Name`, `SourceAddr`/`SourceAddrRaw` (`source_addr`, line 409-411), `DestAddr`/`DestAddrRaw` (`dest_addr`), `LinkBandwidthRaw` (`link_bandwidth`), `LinkRTTRaw` (`link_rtt`). `allowed_ips` is parsed on the **peer**, not the path: `Peer.AllowedIPs []string` at **line 488**. String fields are parsed in `normalize()` (lines 705-761).
- `/home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/internal/config/load.go` — the actual `Load()` entry point (reads file, 0600 mode check, `toml.Unmarshal`, then `normalize`/`validate`).
- **No `bind` or `bind_mode` field exists anywhere** in the config (grep confirmed). There is no per-path/global "mode" concept at all.

### 2. TUN / device creation for wanbond0
- `/home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/internal/device/device.go` — `defaultTUNName = "wanbond0"` (line 40); `Up()` creates it via `tun.CreateTUN(defaultTUNName, tunMTU(cfg))` (**line 189**), then `dev.Up()` (**line 239**) starts the *engine* routines only.
- The daemon does **NOT** bring the link UP and does **NOT** assign addresses/routes — this is explicitly operator-owned (package doc lines 1-8; `tunnel up` log line 289). `dev.Up()` is the amneziawg engine up, not a netlink `IFF_UP`.
- amneziawg-go's `CreateTUN` (in the vendored dep, `.../amneziawg-go/tun/tun_linux.go:551`) sets `IFF_TUN|IFF_NO_PI|IFF_VNET_HDR` but **no `IFF_UP` / `LinkSetUp`** — the interface is created DOWN. **No `TUNSETPERSIST`/`SetPersist` anywhere** (grep of the whole tun package is empty) — the TUN is non-persistent, destroyed when the fd closes.
- **TUN write path / EIO origin**: `NativeTun.Write` at `tun_linux.go:335`, writing each buffer via `tunFile.Write(...)` at `tun_linux.go:359`. A write EIO (classic symptom of writing to a DOWN tun) surfaces up to `.../amneziawg-go/device/receive.go:572` which logs `"Failed to write packets to TUN device: %v"` at Error level. (These are in the dependency at `/nix/store/rwcjd6warwfb6rh3vczslglxgyy5rmpp-wanbond-0.0.0-go-modules/github.com/amnezia-vpn/amneziawg-go/`.)

### 3. Device-bind vs source-bind logic
- `/home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/internal/bind/pathsock.go` — `selectDeviceBinds` (**lines 70-107**), `planPathBinds` (55-68), `interfaceInfo`/`familyBindCount` (device-bind equivalence rules), `listenPath` (33-42, falls back to source-IP bind), `listenOnDevice` (176-203). Comments document `CAP_NET_RAW` requirement (lines 29-30).
- `/home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/internal/bind/pathsock_linux.go` — `bindToDevice()` = `SO_BINDTODEVICE` setsockopt (line 12), needs `CAP_NET_RAW` (line 9).
- `/home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/internal/bind/pathsock_other.go` — non-Linux stub (returns error).
- `/home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/internal/bind/multipath.go` — consumes the plan when opening path sockets (lines ~492-528).

### 4. Metrics (Prometheus)
- `/home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/internal/metrics/metrics.go` — all metric definitions. Per-path: `wanbond_path_tx_bytes_total`, `rx_bytes_total`, `loss_ratio`, `rtt_seconds`, `jitter_seconds`, `throughput_bits_per_second`, `wanbond_path_up` (liveness gauge, `upValue` maps `StateUp→1`). FEC connection-scoped counters (data/repair/recovered/unrecoverable/bytes/residual_loss).
- **No session/handshake-established metric exists** — there is no `wanbond_session_up` or handshake gauge. `wanbond_path_up` is the only liveness signal, and it reflects the probe plane, not WG handshake state.
- `/home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/internal/device/metrics.go` — the `metrics.Source` impl over the Bind (`newMetricsSource`). `/home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/internal/metrics/server.go` — loopback `/metrics` HTTP server; `scrape.go` — scrape helper.

### 5. Logging of the two messages
- `"bind: no healthy path with a known remote endpoint"` — defined as `errNoHealthyPath` in `/home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/internal/bind/multipath.go:64`, returned from `Send()` at **lines 1047 and 1053**. It is returned (not logged) to the amneziawg engine, which logs it via the engine `Errorf` → wanbond `wg.Error` (Error level; adapter in `device.go:687-697` `engineLogger`).
- `"Failed to send handshake initiation"` — emitted by the **dependency** `.../amneziawg-go/device/send.go:195` at Error level (`peer.device.log.Errorf`), routed to wanbond's `wg` component logger at Error. (`"Sending handshake initiation"` is Verbosef/Debug at send.go:119.)

### 6. Handshake / WG session establishment (amneziawg-go integration)
- `/home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/internal/device/failover.go` — `deviceRehandshake()` (**lines 172-194**) is the only place wanbond drives handshakes: `peer.ExpireCurrentKeypairs()` + `peer.SendHandshakeInitiation(false)`. `startHubFailover` (205+) wires it.
- Engine internals (dep): handshake completion observable at `.../amneziawg-go/device/receive.go` (`"Received handshake initiation"` line 414) and `send.go`. **wanbond has no hook that observes handshake completion / emits a "session up" signal today** — the closest existing observable is the Bind learning a path's remote from authenticated traffic (`ps.getRemote()`/setRemote in `internal/bind/multipath.go`, around lines 1050 and 1358-1383). A "session up" signal would be new wiring, likely via a peer event or polling `peer` handshake state in the device package.

### 7. Full-tunnel / allowed_ips / default-route handling
- `allowed_ips` is applied only as WireGuard crypto-routing: rendered into the UAPI string in `/home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/internal/device/device.go` `uapiConfig()` — **lines 732-737** (`allowed_ip=%s`), requiring ≥1 per peer.
- **There is no full-tunnel / default-route / "mode" concept anywhere** (grep for `full.tunnel`, `default route`, `0.0.0.0/0`, `::/0`, `mode` in docs/config/internal returned nothing). Kernel routing and addressing are entirely operator-owned via systemd-networkd (see item 9 §4 / install.md line 608+). `0.0.0.0/0` in `allowed_ips` would be accepted by the engine but wanbond installs no route for it.

### 8. systemd units shipped
- `/home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/packaging/systemd/wanbond-edge.service`
- `/home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/packaging/systemd/wanbond-concentrator.service`
- Both use `Type=exec`, `Restart=on-failure`, `CapabilityBoundingSet=CAP_NET_ADMIN`. **No NetworkManager, oneshot, or `.network` addressing unit is shipped in-repo** — the `/etc/systemd/network/10-wanbond0.network` example lives only inline in `docs/install.md` (§4, lines 608-637); the docs explicitly warn against an `ExecStartPost=ip addr add` drop-in.

### 9. Docs
- `/home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/docs/install.md` — sections: §1 build, §2 install binary, §3 config file (edge/concentrator/`[fec]`/`[scheduler]`, §3a pacing tuning, §3z full key reference), **§4 systemd units** (with the operator-owned "Interface addressing and routing" subsection + the `10-wanbond0.network` example, lines 588-637), §5 firewall, §6 observability, §7 MTU, §8 limitations.
- `/home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/docs/design.md` — architecture (data path, multipath Bind, scheduler, hub failover §219, telemetry, reseq, FEC, load-bearing invariants §343, security §365, boundaries §381).
- `/home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/README.md` — overview, quick start, repo layout, status/limitations.
- `/home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/AGENTS.md` — agent instructions: keep-docs-current rule, build/test/lint, load-bearing invariants, testing discipline, cq planning ledger.
- Other useful docs: `docs/runbook.md`, `docs/manual-checklist.md`, `docs/p1-mtu.md`, `docs/p0-findings.md`, `docs/p0-checkpoint.md`, `docs/fec-baseline.md`.

### 10. Test layout
- Unit/property tests: co-located `*_test.go` throughout `/home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/internal/**` (no build tag; run via `just test` → `go test ./...`).
- netns e2e: `/home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/test/e2e/` — **build tag `//go:build e2e`** (see `doc.go`, `main_test.go`). Privileged (root + CAP_NET_ADMIN + `/dev/net/tun`); run via `just e2e` (`sudo -E go test -tags e2e`). Phases p0-p5 plus failover/roaming/reload/pacing/hub_failover/wireaudit; harness in `netns.go`, `thresholds.go`.
- realhosts tier: `/home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/test/realhosts/` — **build tag `//go:build realhosts`** (fully separate from `e2e`, see `doc.go`). Report-only, drives two standing worker machines over SSH; gated behind `just realhosts` (never in CI). Tiers/tests: `connectivity_test.go` (smoke), `smoke_test.go`, `soak_test.go`, `multipath_failover_test.go`, `wan_kill_failover_test.go`, `aggregation_bufferbloat_test.go`, `provision.go`/`provision_test.go`; reports written to `test/realhosts/reports/` (gitignored).
- Justfile targets: `test` (tagless unit), `e2e`/`e2e-run`, `realhosts`/`realhosts-provision`, plus a p0-baseline orchestration wrapper.
